characters.name: 2-Face-2 (Batman in Bethlehem)
characters.description: 2-Face-2Batman in Bethlehem‎GalleryReal NameUnknownMain Alias2-Face-2Other AliasesJanuaryBase Of OperationsGotham CityStatusAlignmentBadCharacteristicsGenderMaleEyesBlueHairBlackOriginUniverseBatman in BethlehemCreatorsGrant Morrison·Andy KubertFirst AppearanceBatman#700(August, 2010) 2-Face-2is an enemy ofDamian Waynein the future. 2-Face-2 hacked intoGotham City's artificial climate control system, causing it to rain a Monster Serum/Joker Venomhybrid rain. The majority of Gotham's citizens were morphed into crazed Jokerized zombies.

characters.name: 3g4 (New Earth)
characters.description: 3g4Jack Kirby's Fourth WorldGalleryReal Name3g4Main Alias3g4Base Of OperationsApokolipsStatusAlignmentGoodRaceParademonCitizenshipApokoliptianCharacteristicsGenderMaleOriginUniverseFourth World·New EarthCreatorsPeter David·Jim CalafioreFirst AppearanceAppearance of DeathAquamanVol 5  #37(October, 1997)AquamanVol 5  #37(October, 1997) 3g4was aParademonspawned as a soldier onApokolipsduring theGenesisevent. Crash-landing on Earth, he metAquamanand was encouraged to fight against his mindless programming. Developing sentience, he questioned his orders for the first time in Parademon history. His battle-hardened drill instructorTopkickalmost killed Aquaman, but 3g4 distracted him at the last second with an attack to save his new friend's life. Topkick killed 3g4 immediately for incompetence. In his dying breath, Aquaman asked him "Why?" and 3g4 responded "Why... not?" before succumbing to his wounds.

characters.name: 7-Gorn-7 (Teen Titans TV Series)
characters.description: 7-Gorn-7Cartoon Network's Teen Titans‎GalleryReal Name7-Gorn-7Main Alias7-Gorn-7AffiliationControl FreakStatusAlignmentBadIdentityPublic IdentityCharacteristicsEyesNo EyesHairNo HairOriginUniverseTeen Titans (TV Series)CreatorsDavid SlackFirst AppearanceLast Appearance"Episode 257-494"(January 17, 2005)"Calling All Titans"(January, 2006) 7-Gorn-7was a movie creature brought to life by theControl Freak. When the villainControl Freakused his magic remote control to enter the television, theTeen Titansused a special machine to follow him. Being outnumbered, Control Freak recruited three villains from different TV shows to destroy the heroes themselves. One of the villains was a golden robot named 7-Gorn-7.

characters.name: 666 (Earth-22)
characters.description: 666Kingdom Come‎GalleryReal NameUnknownMain Alias666StatusAlignmentBadIdentitySecret IdentityMarital StatusSingleOccupationVigilanteCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBaldOriginUniverseEarth-22CreatorsMark Waid·Alex RossFirst AppearanceAppearance of DeathKingdom Come#1(July, 1996)Kingdom Come#4(August, 1996) 666was a violent vigilante who is described as a "tattooed, self-mutilated man-machine of destruction" that fought with and alongsideMagog's generation of superheroes. Apparently he had no interest in reformation whenSupermanreturned from his self-imposed exile to force the current generation superheroes to voluntarily join theJustice League, so he and many other metahumans were instead incarcerated at theGulagconstructed by Superman and the Justice League to contain and re-educate them.

characters.name: A'Hwiirdh-Paan'A (New Earth)
characters.description: HarpisMainstream Universe‎‎, 1950s-2011‎GalleryReal NameA'Hwiirdh-Paan'AMain AliasHarpisOther AliasesHwiirdh-PaanRelativesDuarmzshee-Paan(sister, deceased)AffiliationOmega MenBase Of OperationsVega star systemStatusAlignmentGoodIdentityPublic IdentityRaceAellanMarital StatusSingleCharacteristicsGenderFemaleHeight5' 6"Weight115 lbs (52 kg)EyesBlueHairNo HairSkinPurpleOriginUniverseEarth-One·New EarthCreatorsMarv Wolfman·Joe StatonFirst AppearanceAppearance of DeathGreen LanternVol 2  #141(June, 1981)Omega MenVol 2  #1(December, 2006) Harpiswas a member of theOmega Men. Harpis was the younger of two sisters from the planetAello. Years ago, Harpis and her sisterDemoniatraveled to the planetRashashoonwhere they became professional "providers" at a bordello in the city of Raggashoon operated by Mama Madame. They were both thrilled with the prospect of earning wealth and power and living in the sort of opulence that only Mama Madame could provide. A'Haarp found herself highly suited to most of Raggashoon's clients and became one of Madame's most popular providers - even more so than her jealous, yet protective sister Demonia. Although Mama Madame usually catered to clients loyal to the Citadel, she also provided companionship to various revolutionaries. During these years, Harpis had relations with the KarnanTigorr, as well as Tigorr's second-in-commandDelengis Jarouq, the latter of whom she fell deeply in love with. Such attachment was strongly discouraged amongst those under Mama Madame's charge and it was made all the worse by the fact that Jarouq was a revolutionary.

characters.name: A-Mortal (New Earth)
characters.description: A-MortalMainstream Universe‎,1986‎-2011‎GalleryReal NameUnknownMain AliasA-MortalAffiliationHyperclanBase Of OperationsZ'onn Z'orrStatusAlignmentBadIdentitySecret IdentityRaceWhite MartianMarital StatusSingleOccupationSuper-VillainCharacteristicsGenderMaleEyesRedHairNo HairSkinWhiteOriginUniverseNew EarthCreatorsGrant Morrison·Howard PorterFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceJLA#1(January, 1997)JLA Secret Files and Origins#1(September, 1997) A-Mortalwas aWhite Martianand a member of the alien super-team known as theHyperclan. A-Mortal and his seven colleagues came toEarthseveral years ago, claiming to be the last survivors of a world that had been destroyed by geological upheaval and environmental irresponsibility. Vowing to never allow such a catastrophe to ever occur again, the Hyperclan allegedly journeyed through space seeking out planets in need of saving. In truth though, this was just a front to turn public opinion against Earth's more prominent super-heroes, specifically, theJustice League of America.

characters.name: A-Mortal (New Earth)

characters.name: A-Train (The Boys)
characters.description: A-TrainThe Boys‎GalleryReal NameUnknownMain AliasA-TrainAffiliationThe Seven· FormerlyTeenage KixStatusAlignmentBadIdentitySecret IdentityCitizenshipAmericanMarital StatusSingleOccupationAdventurerCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBrownOriginUniverseThe BoysCreatorsGarth Ennis·Darick RobertsonFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceThe Boys#1(October, 2006)The Boys#6(February, 2007) A-Trainis a cruel and irresponsible speedster and a member of theSeven. A-Train is a superhero with barely any sense of responsibility. While fighting a supervillain, he flung him in the direction of civilians, which caused the death ofRobin. He left the scene as soon as someone gave him the directions back to theU.S..[1]He sexually assaultedStarlightas her last trial to joinThe Seven[2]and mocked her during an official meeting.[3]As this type of behaviour is condemned during this type of meeting,Homelanderpunched A-Train and threatened to throw him back to theTeenage Kixif he didn't act accordingly.[3]

characters.name: A-Train (The Boys)
characters.description: A-TrainThe Boys‎GalleryReal NameUnknownMain AliasA-TrainAffiliationThe Seven· FormerlyTeenage KixStatusAlignmentBadIdentitySecret IdentityCitizenshipAmericanMarital StatusSingleOccupationAdventurerCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBrownOriginUniverseThe BoysCreatorsGarth Ennis·Darick RobertsonFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceThe Boys#1(October, 2006)The Boys#6(February, 2007) A-Trainis a cruel and irresponsible speedster and a member of theSeven. A-Train is a superhero with barely any sense of responsibility. While fighting a supervillain, he flung him in the direction of civilians, which caused the death ofRobin. He left the scene as soon as someone gave him the directions back to theU.S..[1]He sexually assaultedStarlightas her last trial to joinThe Seven[2]and mocked her during an official meeting.[3]As this type of behaviour is condemned during this type of meeting,Homelanderpunched A-Train and threatened to throw him back to theTeenage Kixif he didn't act accordingly.[3]

characters.name: A.J. (Exterminators)
characters.description: A.J.Exterminators‎GalleryReal NameA.J.Main AliasA.J.AffiliationBug-Bee-GoneBase Of OperationsLos Angeles,CaliforniaStatusAlignmentNeutralIdentityPublic IdentityCitizenshipAmericanMarital StatusSingleOccupationExterminatorCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBrownOriginUniverseExterminatorsCreatorsSimon Oliver·Tony MooreFirst AppearanceAppearance of DeathExterminators#1(March, 2006)Exterminators#3(May, 2006) A.J.was an employee ofBug-Bee-Gone. A.J. was born in the back of a Ford flatbed truck on August 27,1969. A short man with rat-like features, A.J. was a callus and crude individual whose rampant misogyny was equal only to his profane temperament. In the early 1990s, A.J.'s reckless lifestyle caught up with him and was offered one of two choices: Either he could go to prison for a variety of crimes, or he could join theUnited StatesMarine Corps. Opting for the latter, A.J. served a tour in the first Persian Gulf War. He was no more liked amongst his peers in the service than he was by the civilians back home. His fellow soldiers often berated him and put him through a harsh hazing process. During the war, A.J. came into possession of an ornatebox, which contained a raisedNaziseal and a relief of a scarab beetle. He brought the box back to the U.S. and kept it in a secure compartment in the back of his truck.

characters.description: A.J.Exterminators‎GalleryReal NameA.J.Main AliasA.J.AffiliationBug-Bee-GoneBase Of OperationsLos Angeles,CaliforniaStatusAlignmentNeutralIdentityPublic IdentityCitizenshipAmericanMarital StatusSingleOccupationExterminatorCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBrownOriginUniverseExterminatorsCreatorsSimon Oliver·Tony MooreFirst AppearanceAppearance of DeathExterminators#1(March, 2006)Exterminators#3(May, 2006) A.J.was an employee ofBug-Bee-Gone. A.J. was born in the back of a Ford flatbed truck on August 27,1969. A short man with rat-like features, A.J. was a callus and crude individual whose rampant misogyny was equal only to his profane temperament. In the early 1990s, A.J.'s reckless lifestyle caught up with him and was offered one of two choices: Either he could go to prison for a variety of crimes, or he could join theUnited StatesMarine Corps. Opting for the latter, A.J. served a tour in the first Persian Gulf War. He was no more liked amongst his peers in the service than he was by the civilians back home. His fellow soldiers often berated him and put him through a harsh hazing process. During the war, A.J. came into possession of an ornatebox, which contained a raisedNaziseal and a relief of a scarab beetle. He brought the box back to the U.S. and kept it in a secure compartment in the back of his truck.

characters.name: A.J. (New Earth)
characters.description: A.J.Mainstream Universe‎,1986‎-2011‎GalleryReal NameArthur Joseph "A.J." Curry[citation needed]Main AliasA.J.Other AliasesAquamanRelativesMera(mother)Orin(possible father)Thanatos(possible father)Arthur Curry, Jr. (brother, deceased)Koryak(possible half-brother, deceased)Hila(aunt)AffiliationAquaman FamilyStatusAlignmentGoodRaceAtlantean/Homo MagihybridOccupationKingCharacteristicsGenderMaleEyesBlueHairBlondOriginUniverseNew EarthCreatorsPeter David·Martin EgelandFirst AppearanceAquamanVol 5  #11(August, 1995) Arthur Joseph "A.J." Currywas the son ofMera, the wife ofAquaman. He eventually became anAquamanhimself as the protector of the dimensional realm Netherspace, his childhood home. Shortly after the death ofher infant son, Mera was taken byThanatos, in the guise of her love Orin. Approximately nine months afterwards, Mera gave birth to A.J.. While his true father is unknown, it was often assumed to be the real Orin. Raised in Netherspace, the evil purgatory water-world realm Thanatos belonged to, his body was not accustomed to the passage of time in theNew Earthuniverse.

characters.name: A.K. O'Fallon (Earth-Two)
characters.description: A.K. O'FallonMainstream Universe‎, 1930s-1950sGalleryReal NameA.K. O'FallonRelativesSenator O'Fallon(father)Base Of OperationsWashington, D.C.StatusAlignmentBadIdentityPublic IdentityCitizenshipAmericanOccupationNewspaper ownerCharacteristicsGenderMaleHairBrownOriginUniverseEarth-TwoCreatorsRoy Thomas·Dann Thomas·Rafael KayananFirst AppearanceLast AppearanceAmerica vs. the Justice Society#1(January, 1985)America vs. the Justice Society#4(April, 1985) A.K. O'Fallonwas a newspaper magnate, the son of the lateSenator O'Fallon. AK's father Senator O'Fallon was in charge of the Joint Congressional Un-American Activity Committee, which caused theJustice Societyto disband. When he was killed in a fire, A.K. suspected the Justice Society had caused his death as revenge. A.K. became obsessed with his own revenge. His newspaper, theCapital Globe,helped instigate a Congressional hearing into the Justice Society based on events detailed in theBatman diary. He also funded efforts byPer Degaton, though he considered this a backup plan.

A Wolf in Gotham#6(April, 2022) A.S. Scarlet, also known as theBookworm, was aGotham Citycriminal who specialized in literature-themed crimes, making him an enemy ofBatman. He has also been a professor of English literature atGotham Academy.
